role: National Security Advisor (Acting)
note: Assumed the role in an acting capacity 2025-05-01 after Mike Waltz was removed; concurrently remains Secretary of State (first dual-hat since Kissinger). Still acting NSA as of mid-2026 per NPR (2026-03-14); no permanent NSA named.

role: National Security Advisor
note: Original second-term NSA; removed 2025-05-01 in the first major shakeup (amid 'Signalgate') and subsequently moved toward the UN Ambassador post. Included as the notable predecessor.

role: Deputy National Security Advisor
note: Longtime Rubio aide promoted from State Dept (Counselor / Director of Policy Planning) to deputy NSA on 2026-05-26, described by WaPo/Axios as the 'top White House national security post.' Serves alongside other deputy NSAs Andy Baker and Robert Gabriel Jr.; no source uses the literal 'Principal Deputy' title.

role: Deputy National Security Advisor
note: Named deputy NSA in the May 2025 NSC restructuring; concurrently serves as VP JD Vance's national security advisor (since Jan 2025). Included as a co-deputy NSA.

role: Homeland Security Advisor
note: Holds the Homeland Security Advisor title concurrently with White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy since inauguration (same person as wh-deputy-cos-policy).

role: Director of the National Economic Council
note: Still NEC director as of mid-2026. Considered for Fed Chair but Trump nominated Kevin Warsh (Jan 2026) instead, explicitly to keep Hassett at the NEC.

role: Acting Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers
note: CEA Vice Chairman (Feb 2025) who became Acting Chair when Stephen Miran took leave for the Fed Board; remains acting chair pending Senate action on nominee Christopher Phelan.

role: Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers (nominee)
note: University of Minnesota economist nominated 2026-04-21 to chair CEA; Senate Banking Committee confirmation hearing held 2026-06-18. Not yet confirmed as of as_of date.

role: Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers
note: First Trump-2 CEA chair (confirmed mid-March 2025; start date approximate). Took leave Sept 2025 for an interim Federal Reserve Board seat, then formally resigned the CEA chair 2026-02-03 to remain at the Fed. Notable predecessor.

role: Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy
note: Senate-confirmed OSTP director (74-25) on 2025-03-25; still serving in 2026.

role: Chair of the Council on Environmental Quality
note: 13th CEQ chair; acting from 2025-01-20, Senate-confirmed mid-September 2025.

role: White House AI and Crypto Czar (Special Advisor for AI and Crypto)
note: Left the czar role late March 2026 after exhausting his 130-day special-government-employee limit, moving to co-chair PCAST. No successor named; the AI and Crypto Czar position is vacant as of the as_of date.

role: Counsel to the President (White House Counsel)
White House Press Secretary
White House Communications Director
White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy
note: Concurrently serves as Homeland Security Advisor (see wh-homeland-security-advisor).

role: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Operations
note: William 'Beau' Harrison; serving since Jan 2025 through mid-2026.

role: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Legislative, Political and Public Affairs (Acting)
note: Former RNC chief of staff who joined the White House ~2026-05-01 to fill this role while James Blair is on leave running Trump's 2026 midterm operation; Wikipedia/LegiStorm describe him as acting.

role: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Legislative, Political and Public Affairs
note: Took a leave of absence (~April 2026) to run Trump's 2026 midterm political operation; retains an Assistant to the President title while Richard Walters fills the role in an acting capacity. Some June 2026 sources still attribute the title to Blair, so 'former' reflects his stepping away from active duties rather than a clean departure.

role: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Strategic Implementation
note: Still holds the role as of late June 2026, but reported (June 2026) to be slated to become VP JD Vance's chief of staff around August 2026 — record may go stale shortly after the as_of date.

role: White House Staff Secretary
White House Cabinet Secretary
note: Held the dual title 'Deputy Chief of Staff for Communications and Personnel, and Cabinet Secretary' from inauguration; departed by 2025-10-01 (announced 2025-09-24, Axios). No clearly named successor as Cabinet Secretary was found and the related Director of Cabinet Affairs (Lea Bardon) also left ~April 2026, so the Cabinet Secretary role appears vacant/absorbed as of the as_of date — included only as the sourced former holder.

role: Director of the Office of National Drug Control Policy (Drug Czar)
note: Nominated March 2025; Senate-confirmed 52-48 on 2026-01-06 as the 10th ONDCP Director and first woman to lead the office.

role: Director of the Office of Presidential Personnel
note: Took over PPO on 2025-10-13 after Sergio Gor was confirmed Ambassador to India; concurrently remains a White House Deputy Chief of Staff (Assistant to the President) since 2025-01-20.

role: Director of the Office of Presidential Personnel
note: Led PPO from inauguration; nominated U.S. Ambassador to India (Aug 2025) and confirmed, ending PPO tenure (~Oct 2025); serving as Ambassador to India since 2026.

role: Director of the Domestic Policy Council
note: DPC Director since inauguration; still serving in 2026.

role: Director of the White House Office of Public Liaison
note: Deputy Assistant to the President and Director of the Office of Public Liaison since Jan 2025; still listed as current in 2026 (LegiStorm; Punchbowl 2026 Power List).

role: Senior Counselor to the President for Trade and Manufacturing
note: Assistant to the President and Senior Counselor for Trade and Manufacturing; in office since inauguration.

role: Senior Advisor to the President (de facto head of DOGE)
note: Served as Senior Advisor and de facto DOGE head as a special government employee; departed late May 2025 when the 130-day SGE limit expired (sources vary May 28 vs May 30, 2025). Included as a notable former senior advisor.
